sections.paragraph:
μένω [Skt. assoc.]

—1. ‘be in a situation for a length of time’, remain, stay, intr.

—a. of being in a location, whether geographical site, dwelling, or person, or thing Mt 10:11; Mk 14:34; Lk 8:27; J 7:9; 8:31; 12:46.

—b. of continuing in a state or condition

—α. w. focus on sameness J 12:24; Ac 27:41; 1 Cor 7:20.

—β. w. focus on existence J 12:34; 1 Cor 15:6; Phil 1:25; Hb 7:24; 1 J 2:17; Rv 17:10.

—2. ‘stay in a place for the presence/arrival of’ someone, await, wait for, tr. Ac 20:5, 23.
